    Dave K
    57 months ago
    Finally an iPhone that would make Jobs proud.
    I’m so glad I went with the Air. I’ve been a pro max user since the iPhone 12. I’m not a YouTube influencer, I don’t use my phone to record content in ... More
    I’m so glad I went with the Air. I’ve been a pro max user since the iPhone 12. I’m not a YouTube influencer, I don’t use my phone to record content in astronomicaly large file sizes to edit and transfer to a computer to post on my YouTube channel. Don’t buy into all the influencer hype. I always went with the pro max because it was the only way to get the bigger screen, 120 hz pro motion and always on display, faster chip and ram and larger base storage. That’s what was important to me with the way I use my phone. I couldn’t care less about cameras. Apple changed all that this year. Now I get all that I need in the Air without having to shell out the cash for the pro max for camera features I never used. You get the perfect screen size, 120 pro motion, always on display, 12 gig ram and 256 base storage in a super slim comfortable device that’s awesome to hold and slip in and out of pockets. The camera is awesome, more than most people need. I think this is the first real innovation we’ve seen from apple since the iPhone 10 and I’m all for it. These pro models just keep getting bigger, thicker, heavier and gaudier every single year just to appease the social media people. The 17 pro models, in my opinion, are the ugliest design I’ve ever seen in an iPhone. Wasn’t it just a few years ago aluminum was junk? That’s why they went to stainless steel and then titanium? Now we’re back to aluminum? I’ll take the titanium Air. I’ve had this phone for over a week now and the performance is fantastic, and yes I’m getting ALL DAY LONG out of the battery. 5.5 to 6 hours screen time no issues. All while feeling like I’m holding a piece of the future in my hands. I can’t put the thing down, probably not a good thing. Anyone on the fence shouldn’t be. Don’t listen to anybody else, try it for yourself. I personally am not going back. Less
    Reviewed on vzw.com
    Provide feedback
    Justin
    58 months ago
    Goodbye Pro, Hello Air: The Perfect iPhone Yet... for me.
    TL;DR This is officially my go-to model moving forward. The combination of lightness, design, battery life, camera, and tech performance makes the iPhone ... More
    TL;DR This is officially my go-to model moving forward. The combination of lightness, design, battery life, camera, and tech performance makes the iPhone Air the most enjoyable iPhone I’ve owned. The details; I upgrade my iPhone every year (yes, I’m one of those people), and I’ve always leaned toward the Pro models—my last being the iPhone 16 Pro, and I’ve owned every Pro before that. That said, the new iPhone Air is hands down my favorite iPhone so far, with the iPhone X still holding second place. I went with Space Black this time (usually go white or titanium), and it looks incredibly sleek on the Air. The design is thin, modern, and what really stands out is how light and “airy” it feels in the hand. Coming from a Pro, I was worried about trade-offs, but after a week of use, here’s what I’ve noticed: Battery Life: About the same as my 16 Pro. Cameras: The selfie camera is noticeably better, and the main cameras handle my everyday use just fine. Screen: Perfect size—right between a standard/Pro and a Plus/Max. Crisp display with excellent anti-glare (first time I’ve skipped a screen protector because of this). Weight: The difference is real—much lighter and less bulky than the Pro line. I did pick up the $99 MagSafe Battery Pack (great for travel or tossing in a backpack). It does make the phone heavier when attached, but I only use it occasionally. I grabbed a bumper for some light protection while I adjust, but usually go with no case. Less
    Reviewed on vzw.com
